name: SIEM Lab - Root Login Success
description: Flags successful root logins so the lab can produce benign-but-suspicious alerts.
author: Codex
false_positives: Approved break-glass maintenance from a trusted bastion.

name: SIEM Lab - Web Login Failures
description: Flags repeated login failures seen through the lab web gateway.
query: event.dataset: "lab.web" and url.path: "/rest/user/login" and http.response.status_code >= 400 and not user_agent.original: "TrustedScanner/1.0"
author: Codex
false_positives: Load tests or intentionally noisy authentication probes.

name: SIEM Lab - Web Exploit Probe
description: Detects exploit-like requests and scanner fingerprints in the lab web telemetry.
query: event.dataset: "lab.web" and (url.path: "/ftp/package.json.bak" or url.path: "/assets/etc/passwd" or url.query: "*or%201=1--*" or user_agent.original: "sqlmap/1.8") and not user_agent.original: "TrustedScanner/1.0"
author: Codex
false_positives: Approved security scanning from a trusted source when allowlisting is missing.

name: SIEM Lab - Reverse Shell Command
description: Detects reverse-shell style command lines in Linux process telemetry.
author: Codex

name: SIEM Lab - Encoded PowerShell Command
description: Detects encoded PowerShell execution in replayed Windows telemetry.
author: Codex
false_positives: Some administrative automation, though it should still be reviewed.
